title: [Bug]: Set-ADAccountPasswordHash breaks Kerberos in Windows Server 2025 after June 2026 patches
author: NikiforovLancloudKZ
body:
### Bug Description

This was seen after installing latest June2026 patch on Win2025 domain controller, when domain and forest is also on Win2025 mode.
After setting NTHash manually, login using Kerberos using correct password (matching provided hash) is failed.
Seemingly this happens because of two factors:
. Proposed removing of RC4 support in Kerberos, although Microsoft wants to force remove RC4 only in July patch.
. Clearing of SupplementalCredentials fields in Get-ADReplAccount, after setting hash with Set-ADAccountPasswordHash.

These fields display empty values such as:
 SupplementalCredentials:
    ClearText:
    NTLMStrongHash:
    Kerberos:
    KerberosNew:
    WDigest:
but when resetting account password manually, SupplementalCredentials are set.

User authentication is restored after setting a registry key proposed by Microsoft - RC4DefaultDisablementPhase - back to legacy mode. But, as Microsoft claims, in July patch this registry key will be ignored and new policy is forced.

Interestingly enough, on Win2022 domains, and on Win2019 domains, authentication after setting password hash works correctly, Problem arises only on Win2025 domain controllers win Win2025 domain mode. No other software exists on DC, just freshly installed Windows 2025 with latest June patch. Before June patch all worked correctly.

Could it be possible for command Set-ADAccountPasswordHash to not to clear Kerberos keys or regenerate them using NTHash? 

### Steps to Reproduce

Win2025 domain controller, Win2025 forest/domain mode

Get-ADReplAccount -samaccountname username -server DC
note NTHash value
Set-ADAccountPasswordHash -samaccountname username -NTHash same_hash_as_above -server DC
then for example "runas /user:DOMAINNAME\username cmd" using the same password - consistent with same hash - returns User name or password is incorrect.

### Expected Behavior

As before, when replacing user NTHash with known password hash (for example synchronizing it from another AD deployment) should allow user login using same password consistent with his NTHash.

### Actual Behavior

command executes without error but user login after that returns Username or password is incorrect.


### Stack Trace

```text

```

### DSInternals Module Version

7.0

### PowerShell Version

5.1.26100.32995

### Operating System

Windows Server 2025

### Target Domain Controller Version

Windows Server 2025

### Processor Architecture

x64

### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the existing issues to make sure this bug has not already been reported.
- [x] I have tested with the latest version of the DSInternals PowerShell module.

title: [Bug]:
author: RousePartnersLLP-DB
body:
### Bug Description

No results under heading "Passwords of these accounts have been found in the dictionary:"

I have downloaded the NTLM encrypred "have I been pwned" database as a text file using the provided dotnot module from haveibeenpwned. Script used below.

I intentionally have a low priority account in the environment with a known "pwned" password and it does not appear in the results. I can run the script replacing the massive results list with a single line file with the pwned password in plain text, and it appears when I run the script again.

### Steps to Reproduce

$Passwords = "pwnedpasswords_ntlm.txt"

$Params = @{
"All"         = $True
"Server"      = 'NAMEOFSERVER'
"NamingContext" = 'dc=DOMAIN,dc=NAME'
}

Get-ADReplAccount @Params | Test-PasswordQuality -WeakPasswordsFile $Passwords -IncludeDisabledAccounts

### Expected Behavior

Normal expected results of the script, but with at least 1 account listed under section "Passwords of these accounts have been found in the dictionary:"

### Actual Behavior

Passwords of these accounts have been found in the dictionary:
(Blank, no names or accounts found)

### Stack Trace

```text
There is no error or stack trace, the script appears to run normally, and outputs in other areas are within expected norms.
```

### DSInternals Module Version

7.0

### PowerShell Version

5.1.26100.8655

### Operating System

Microsoft Windows 11 Enterprise

### Target Domain Controller Version

Windows Server 2022

### Processor Architecture

x64

### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the existing issues to make sure this bug has not already been reported.
- [x] I have tested with the latest version of the DSInternals PowerShell module.

title: [Bug]: Delegation might be limited by Protected Users Group too
author: aseques
body:
### Bug Description

Since windows 2012r2 there's a group called Protected Users that, when added to the user adds some security restrictions. It's recommended that the admins are part of this group (since they're not supposed to be running regular software)

The Test-PasswordQuality is adding those users in the block "These administrative accounts (current or former ones) are allowed to be delegated:"

### Documentation 

- Docs about current state of the setting https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/ad-ds/manage/how-to-configure-protected-accounts
- The protected group documentation [here](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/security/credentials-protection-and-management/protected-users-security-group)

### Steps to Reproduce

- Member of admin group
- Member of protected users group
- Not having checked the "Account is sensitive and cannot be delegated" setting

### Expected Behavior

Account should be safe from Delegation attacks, so there shouldn't be any warnings in the block "These administrative accounts (current or former ones) are allowed to be delegated"

### Actual Behavior

Users in the protected group are in the warning

### Stack Trace

```text

```

### DSInternals Module Version

6.4

### PowerShell Version

7.5.4

### Operating System

Microsoft Windows Server 2025 Datacenter

### Target Domain Controller Version

Microsoft Windows Server 2025 Datacenter

### Processor Architecture

x64

### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the existing issues to make sure this bug has not already been reported.
- [x] I have tested with the latest version of the DSInternals PowerShell module.

title: [Bug]: german umlauts in sites
author: JobstF
body:
### Bug Description

A company I support has a site "CN=Löhne,CN=Sites,CN=Configuration,DC=XXXX,DC=local".

When I run "Get-ADReplAccount -all -NamingContext $ADDomain.DistinguishedName -Server $env:COMPUTERNAME" against this domain, the cmdlet fails with the following message, and I cannot retrieve the entire domain:

"Get-ADReplAccount : Object with identity 'CN=142SRV-DC,CN=Servers,CN=L\C3\B6hne,CN=Sites,CN=Configuration,DC=XXX,DC=local' has not been found."

We don't need to discuss the fact that the use of umlauts in Active Directory domains, and especially when naming sites, should be avoided.

### Steps to Reproduce

Run "Get-ADReplAccount -all -NamingContext $ADDomain.DistinguishedName -Server $env:COMPUTERNAME" against a domain with german umlauts in Site names.

### Expected Behavior

No problems, when running against AD-Domains with german umlauts in Sites.

### Actual Behavior

see description.

### Stack Trace

```text

```

### DSInternals Module Version

6.4

### PowerShell Version

5.1.26100.32522

### Operating System

Windows Server 2025 and before

### Target Domain Controller Version

Windows Server 2025 and before

### Processor Architecture

x64

### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the existing issues to make sure this bug has not already been reported.
- [x] I have tested with the latest version of the DSInternals PowerShell module.

title: [Question]: SupportsKerberosAESEncryption returns false as default
author: HoffmannTom
body:
### Question

Hello,
the method SupportsKerberosAESEncryption() returns false if the SupportedEncryptionTypes-field is empty.
As far as I know, this doesn't mean that AES is not supported. It just means, it supports all encryptions that are available domain-wide. This includes AES encryption for currently all supported windows server versions.

### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the existing issues and documentation for an answer to my question.

title: [Bug]: Arithmetic Overflow Exception in
author: BECOPRO-IT
body:
### Bug Description

I using a powershell script Get-pwnedpasswords and it looks like:

```
"Getting leaked passwords used in your Active Directory domain"

Import-Module "DSinternals" -ErrorAction Stop

#region important variables
# path to downloaded ntlm ordered database of passwords from https://haveibeenpwned.com/Passwords
$pwnedPasswords = "C:\Upload\ADAudit\pwned-passwords-ntlm-ordered-by-hash.txt" 

# path to txt file with manually entered passwords you want to check too (one password at a line)
$weakPasswords = "C:\Upload\ADAudit\weakPasswordsFile.txt"

# path to exported SYSTEM registry that you've got on DC by running ntdsutil command in previous step
$key = Get-BootKey -SystemHivePath 'C:\Upload\ADAudit\registry\SYSTEM'
if (!$key) { throw "Couldn't get syskey" }

# path to exported ntds.dit password database that you've got on DC by running ntdsutil command in previous step
$ntds = 'C:\Upload\ADAudit\Active Directory\ntds.dit'
#endregion important variables

# parameters for Test-PasswordQuality
$params = @{
    WeakPasswordHashesSortedFile = $pwnedPasswords
}
if ($weakPasswords) {
    $params.WeakPasswordsFile = $weakPasswords
}

# run passwords audit
$result = Get-ADDBAccount -All -DatabasePath $ntds -BootKey $key | Test-PasswordQuality @params

# to get nice human readable output
return ($result | Out-String)

```

A few weeks ago I downloaded the pwned ntlm passwords database and exported our NTLM hashes from the local AD via

`ntdsutil "activate instance ntds" ifm "create full C:\ADAudit"`

I executed the script and and it works.

Today I exported the AD database again and used the same password hash database. I run the script again and got an overflow message:

```
PS C:\upload\ADAudit> .\Get-pwnedpasswords.ps1
Getting leaked passwords used in your Active Directory domain
Test-PasswordQuality : Die arithmetische Operation hat einen Überlauf verursacht.
In C:\upload\ADAudit\Get-pwnedpasswords.ps1:29 Zeichen:68
+ ... -All -DatabasePath $ntds -BootKey $key | Test-PasswordQuality @params
+                                              ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    + CategoryInfo          : NotSpecified: (:) [Test-PasswordQuality], OverflowException
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : System.OverflowException,DSInternals.PowerShell.Commands.TestPasswordQualityCommand
```

Used version:

```
PS C:\upload\ADAudit> Get-Command Test-PasswordQuality | Select-Object Name, ModuleName, Version, Source

Name                 ModuleName  Version Source
----                 ----------  ------- ------
Test-PasswordQuality DSinternals 6.3     DSinternals
```

Is this a bug? 




### Steps to Reproduce

1. Export AD database via ntdsutil "activate instance ntds" ifm "create full C:\ADAudit"
2. Run the powershell script

### Expected Behavior

- No errors occured durign running the script

### Actual Behavior

Overflow Exception occured in Test-PasswordQuality

### Stack Trace

```text
Test-PasswordQuality : Die arithmetische Operation hat einen Überlauf verursacht.
In C:\upload\ADAudit\Get-pwnedpasswords.ps1:29 Zeichen:68
+ ... -All -DatabasePath $ntds -BootKey $key | Test-PasswordQuality @params
+                                              ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    + CategoryInfo          : NotSpecified: (:) [Test-PasswordQuality], OverflowException
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : System.OverflowException,DSInternals.PowerShell.Commands.TestPasswordQualityCommand
```

### DSInternals Module Version

6.3

### PowerShell Version

5.1.26100.7705

### Operating System

Windows 11 Pro 25H2 26200.7840

### Target Domain Controller Version

Windows Server 2022

### Processor Architecture

x64

### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the existing issues to make sure this bug has not already been reported.
- [x] I have tested with the latest version of the DSInternals PowerShell module.

title: System.UnauthorizedAccessException: Access is denied   ---> System.ComponentModel.Win32Exception: Access is denied
author: Lakshman-midhyakula
body:
Hi team,

I’m running a .NET application under a dedicated service account, and the account has been granted the following Active Directory replication permissions at the domain root:

- Replicating Directory Changes
- Replicating Directory Changes All
- Replicating Directory Changes In Filtered Set

However, when the application initializes the replication client, it fails immediately at the constructor level with the following exception:

```
System.UnauthorizedAccessException: Access is denied 
 ---> System.ComponentModel.Win32Exception: Access is denied
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at DSInternals.Common.Validator.AssertSuccess(Win32ErrorCode code)
   at DSInternals.Replication.Interop.DrsConnection.Bind(IntPtr rpcHandle)
   at DSInternals.Replication.Interop.DrsConnection..ctor(IntPtr rpcHandle, Guid clientDsa)
   at DSInternals.Replication.DirectoryReplicationClient..ctor(String server, RpcProtocol protocol, NetworkCredential credential)
```

This happens during:

```
var credential = new NetworkCredential(username, password);
using (var client = new DirectoryReplicationClient(server, RpcProtocol.TCP, credential))
{
    // Fails here
}
```

**Observations from our environment:**

LDAP bind using the same credentials succeeds.
RPC connectivity to the domain controller is reachable.
The service account is not a member of privileged groups like Domain Admins.
Required replication permissions are present and verified via ACLs.
The failure occurs during DRS bind (constructor), not during a replication call.

Could you please advise what additional checks or configuration might cause the DRS bind to be rejected at this stage despite the replication permissions being granted?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

Regards,
Lakshman M

### Steps to Reproduce

Unable to reproduce at my end but in customer case always happens

### Expected Behavior

Application should work and return the NTLM hash for a user passed or requested.

### Actual Behavior

Getting exception at the construtor level

### Stack Trace

```text
2026-02-10 10:49:39.895 -05:00 [ERR] ***EXCEPTION***
System.UnauthorizedAccessException: Access is denied ---> System.ComponentModel.Win32Exception: Access is denied
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at DSInternals.Common.Validator.AssertSuccess(Win32ErrorCode code)
   at DSInternals.Replication.Interop.DrsConnection.Bind(IntPtr rpcHandle)
   at DSInternals.Replication.Interop.DrsConnection..ctor(IntPtr rpcHandle, Guid clientDsa)
   at DSInternals.Replication.DirectoryReplicationClient..ctor(String server, RpcProtocol protocol, NetworkCredential credential)
   at SureMDMAdService.Services.AdService.<FullSync>d__111.MoveNext()
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.TaskAwaiter.HandleNonSuccessAndDebuggerNotification(Task task)
   at SureMDMAdService.Services.AdService.<HandleSyncUsers>d__110.MoveNext()
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.TaskAwaiter.HandleNonSuccessAndDebuggerNotification(Task task)
```

### DSInternals Module Version

6.3

### PowerShell Version

5.1.17763.2931

### Operating System

Microsoft Windows Server 2019 Standard

### Target Domain Controller Version

_No response_

### Processor Architecture

x64

### Checklist

- [x] I have searched the existing issues to make sure this bug has not already been reported.
- [x] I have tested with the latest version of the DSInternals PowerShell module.

title: Format-Custom -View new style and Array export.
author: EamonnDevPowershell
body:
Before Version 5.0 I could do something like.

$results = New-Object System.Collections.ArrayList
$results = 	Get-ADDBAccount -All -DatabasePath 	$PathNTDS"ntds.dit" -LogPath $PathNTDS -BootKey $BootKey
 $results  | Format-Custom -View PwDump 			| Out-File -FilePath "E:\users.pwdump.csv" -Encoding ascii
 $results  | Format-Custom -View JohnNT 			| Out-File -FilePath "E:\users.JohnNT.csv" -Encoding ascii
 
 In 5.0 and above I can do
 Get-ADDBAccount -All -DatabasePath $PathNTDS"ntds.dit" -BootKey $BootKey -ExportFormat PwDump | Where-Object SamAccountType -eq User| Out-File -FilePath "E:\users.pwdump.csv" -Encoding ascii
 Get-ADDBAccount -All -DatabasePath $PathNTDS"ntds.dit" -BootKey $BootKey -ExportFormat JohnNT | Where-Object SamAccountType -eq User| Out-File -FilePath "E:\users.JohnNT.csv" -Encoding ascii
  
  How can I export from the array $results to JohnNT ?
  The reason I ask is that I prune users from the array $results over time and simply want to dump the remainder users to JohnNT.

title: Authenticode signature failure for v6.2.0 release
author: ralish
body:
The v6.2.0 release doesn't have an Authenticode signature which breaks install and update using PowerShellGet cmdlets:

```
PS C:\Windows\system32> Install-Module -Name DSInternals -Verbose
PackageManagement\Install-Package : The module 'DSInternals' cannot be installed or updated because the authenticode signature of the file 'DSInternals.cat' is not valid.
At C:\Program Files\WindowsPowerShell\Modules\PowerShellGet\1.0.0.1\PSModule.psm1:1809 char:21
+ ...          $null = PackageManagement\Install-Package @PSBoundParameters
+                      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    + CategoryInfo          : InvalidOperation: (Microsoft.Power....InstallPackage:InstallPackage) [Install-Package], Exception
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : InvalidAuthenticodeSignature,ValidateAndGet-AuthenticodeSignature,Microsoft.PowerShell.PackageManagement.Cmdlets.InstallPackage
```

I've verified this is the case by manually downloading the new release and inspecting the security catalogue in the `.nupkg`. The immediately prior release, v6.1.1, does have a valid signature on its security catalogue.

The error can be overridden using the `-SkipPublisherCheck` parameter, but that's obviously not ideal, particularly given previous releases did have a valid signature.

title: format-custom views not working.
author: jesperprintz
body:
I have an old server where i run Dsinternals 4.14, which works.

i run a Get-ADReplAccount and get all acccounts in to a array, loop each user object it into a "Format-Custom -View PwDump", this works as expected, i get a nice single line pr user which i can use.

I also have a new server which is where i am moving it. This runs 6.11. Here the view does not work. 

Get-ADReplAccount works fine  on my 6.11.so module is loaded, but "Format-Custom -View PwDump" will just output the whole object again.

There is no error if i try to use a non existing view, unlike the old 4.11 where i get a error and a nice list of possible views.

The xml files are there fine in the modules folder and i can see the PwDump view is in both versions.

Thanks in advance.

title: Problem with filtering and hashcatnt export
author: tuxbuster
body:
Hello,

The New CmdLets work differently than the old ones (2.16.1). 
How is it possible to filter for all Users in a specific OU and get that as HashCatNT output?

```
### Old Way -> does not work anymore -> no view change
	$RESULT = Get-ADDBAccount -All -BootKey $key -DatabasePath $NTDS | ? DistinguishedName -like '*OU=DD123,DC=CC123,DC=BB123,DC=AA123'
	$RESULT | Format-Custom -View hashcatnt | Out-File $RESULT_FILE_HASHCATNT

### New Way -> doesnt work. No Results
	Get-ADDBAccount -All -BootKey $key -DatabasePath $NTDS -ExportFormat HashcatNT | ? DistinguishedName -like '*OU=DD123,DC=CC123,DC=BB123,DC=AA123'
	
### prefered ways if it would work
	
	### Way 1 - but not wildcards are allowed for DN
	Get-ADDBAccount -BootKey $key -DatabasePath $NTDS -ExportFormat HashcatNT -DistinguishedName '*OU=DD123,DC=CC123,DC=BB123,DC=AA123'
	
	### Way 2 - the switch doesnt exist at the moment
	Get-ADDBAccount -BootKey $key -DatabasePath $NTDS -ExportFormat HashcatNT -Searchbase 'OU=DD123,DC=CC123,DC=BB123,DC=AA123'
```

```
Get-ADDBAccount : Unable to cast object of type 'System.Byte[]' to type 'System.String'.
At line:1 char:1
+ Get-ADDBAccount -key $key -DatabasePath $ntds -all
+ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    + CategoryInfo          : NotSpecified: (:) [Get-ADDBAccount], InvalidCastException
    + FullyQualifiedErrorId : System.InvalidCastException,DSInternals.PowerShell.Commands.GetADDBAccountCommand
```


I have tried it on Windows Server 2022 (Core) and Windows Server 2025 (Core). The Database was merged, defaged, recovered and so on.
With Version 4.xx all worked fine.

title: Format-Custom not working in version 5.1
author: greggbranham
body:
In version 4.7, the command **Get-ADReplAccount -All -Server SERVERNAME | Format-Custom -View PWDump** works fine and produces the correctly formatted output.

In version 5.1, the Format-Custom parameter is ignored regardless of which view is specified.  In all cases, I get output with no formatting.  It seems in version 5.1, the "DSInternals.DSAccount.ExportViews.format.ps1xml" file is ignored and not used.

title: ChangeBootKey issue in Windows 2022
author: LDAPAngel
body:
Hi Michael,

Have a really weird issue that I just cannot get to the bottom of, which is happening for Win2022 & Win2025 only

I'm getting the online bootkey by using

byte[] OnlineBootKey = BootKeyRetriever.GetBootKey();

and the offline bootkey from the IFM registry using

byte[] IFMBootKey = BootKeyRetriever.GetBootKey(@"c:\ifm\registry\SYSTEM");

I then set this in the DIT using


DirectoryContext directoryContext = new DirectoryContext(@"C:\IFM\Active Directory\ntds.dit", false, @"C:\IFM\Active Directory");    
DirectoryAgent directoryAgent = new DirectoryAgent(directoryContext);

directoryAgent.ChangeBootKey(IFMBootKey, OnlineBootKey);

directoryAgent.Dispose();
directoryContext.Dispose();
directoryAgent = null;
directoryContext = null;

After this the LSA policy is reset using c# code

ADDS is then started and for any OS up to and including 2019, this works fine, the DIT comes online

However, for 2022 and 2025, I'm getting a BSOD

But and here is the weird thing

If I use Set-ADDSBootKey rather than C# and supply the old & new bootkeys,  LSA policy is reset using the same c# code and ADDS will start successfully.

I cannot see why its not working for 2022/2025, as looking at the powershell for Set-ADDSBootKey, I'm doing exactly the same in c#.

I've tried various versions, from 4.13 to 5.1 and same issue with 2022/2025 (btw 32kb page size not enabled on 2025).

Any idea on what I'm doing wrong ?


**PEK education**

Also I looked into the PEKlist attribute when the bootkey is changed and don't understand the logic below (this was done on a 2019 OS)

Using an IFM, I set the bootkey from the IFM registry (ie old) bootkey to the online (new) bootkey using c# code as above
Look at the PEKlist and dump the byte data

I can load up the DIT and secrets are decoded correctly to their hashes (unicode p/w etc)


I then repeat the above (after reverting IFM back) , but this time use Set-ADDBBootKey to change the keys.
Look at the PEKlist and dump the byte data

I can load up the DIT and secrets are decoded correctly to their hashes (unicode p/w etc)

However looking at the 2 PEKs, they are different, I was expecting them to be the same, can you explain the logic here ?
